title: Anacis auriculata
taxonomy: Anacis auriculata belongs to the family Achariaceae, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. The classification of Anacis auriculata further places it within the order Malpighiales. The family Achariaceae is a relatively small group, containing around 200 species spread across 4 genera. Anacis auriculata, along with other species in the family, is known for its distinctive flowers and unique growth habits. Further research is needed to fully understand the taxonomic classification of Anacis auriculata.

benefits: Anacis auriculata has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, and its supposed health benefits include reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. However, more studies are needed to confirm the full extent of its health benefits.
